claim: GWAS on 2,155 dog genomes identified 11 genetic loci strongly associated with behavior, none of which were specific to any breed, suggesting behavioral traits predate modern canine breeds by thousands of years.
stance_target: breed as primary driver of behavioral genetics
scope_conditions: 2,155 dog genomes, 18,385 owner surveys, 78 breeds, Darwin's Ark; published Science 2022, DOI: 10.1126/science.abk0639
quoted_support: 'The majority of behaviors that we think of as characteristics of specific modern dog breeds have most likely come about from thousands of years of evolution from wolf to wild canine to domesticated dog, and finally to modern breeds,' said Karlsson.

claim: Physical traits are more than 5x more likely to be predicted by breed than behavioral traits.
stance_target: equivalence of physical and behavioral breed predictability
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022, GWAS of 2,155 genomes; breed ancestry vs. trait correlation analysis
quoted_support: Physical traits like coat color were more than five times more likely to be predicted by breed than behavioral traits.

claim: Biddability (response to human direction) is more heritable and breed-correlated than agonistic threshold (ease of provocation), meaning breed priors are weakest for fear/reactivity prediction in rescue contexts.
stance_target: differential usefulness of breed priors across behavioral dimensions
scope_conditions: Morrill et al. 2022; analysis of mixed-breed genetic ancestry vs. behavioral trait heritability
quoted_support: For less heritable, less breed differentiated traits, like agonistics threshold (which measures how easily a dog is provoked by frightening, uncomfortable, or annoying stimuli), breed was almost useless as a predictor of behavior.

claim: Even in Labrador Retrievers (lowest propensity to howl), 8% of owners reported their dog sometimes howls, illustrating that breed cannot predict individual behavioral absence.
scope_conditions: Darwin's Ark survey data; Morrill et al. 2022
quoted_support: Even in Labrador retrievers, which had the lowest propensity for howling, 8 percent of owners reported their Labrador's sometimes howl.

claim: Modern dog breed physical standardization began in the Victorian era (~1800s), meaning only 150 years of intentional breed-specific physical selection; behavioral traits reflect much older evolutionary pressures.
stance_target: explanation for why breed explains little behavioral variance
scope_conditions: Historical framing from Morrill et al. 2022; wolves to dogs ~10,000-15,000 years ago, work-role breeding ~2,000 years ago, Victorian physical standardization ~150 years ago
quoted_support: It was not until the Victorian era in the 1800s that humans began selecting dogs consistently for the physical and aesthetic traits that today we commonly associate with modern breeds.
